title: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 31 ending and why Asuka is NOT on the train platform
display_title: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 31 ending and why Asuka is NOT on the train platform
wikitext:
[]: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Sat Dec 21, 2024 7:48 am One thing about the ending of Shin Evangelion I could never understand: if what we see is happening in the Minus-space, why is character strongly resembling Asuka is sitting in the train station, near Kaworu and Rei, when Asuka was ejected from the minus space in her pod? And if it is in fact Asuka, why Mari and Shinji run away from the train station, not acknowledging her presence? I think I finally found the answer. When Asuka turned into angel, her original’s soul pulled her out of EVA 02, and transferred her soul into EVA 13, preventing her from dying from her chocker. It means that both Asuka’s soul and Asuka’s original soul were placed inside EVA 13 from that moment. When EVA 13 entered the Minus-space, both Asuka's soul, and her original's soul ended up in the Minus-space. I always wondered what happened to the soul of Asuka's original. For those who don’t know, Asuka’s original is the DNA donor of the Shikinami series clones. Her soul was a part of EVA 13 or EVA 02 interface. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sun Dec 22, 2024 6:42 am This is a pretty good theory. I think Asuka original being in the train station would be a reasonable explanation. Honestly, I've also had this theory. That said, I should mention the script for the train platform scene mentions Rei and Kaworu, but not Asuka. That makes me think the redhead woman was just a random passenger (as other characters in the train station). It would explain why the scene never focus on her. Maybe the redhead woman ressembling Asuka was just a reference from the animators.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Sun Dec 22, 2024 9:05 am Konja7 wrote: That said, I should mention the script for the train platform scene mentions Rei and Kaworu, but not Asuka. That makes me think the redhead woman was just a random passenger (as other characters in the train station). It would explain why the scene never focus on her. Maybe the redhead woman ressembling Asuka was just a reference from the animators.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Allansfirebird » Wed Dec 25, 2024 11:58 pm Konja7#944154 wrote: If we look closely, that person VERY much looks like Asuka, same hair-color, hair-style and red phone and backpack. Red phone is another key that this is not Asuka, because Asuka had a game console that is white. So if she looks like Asuka, but not actually Asuka, that person could only be a perfect genetic copy of Asuka: her original's soul.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Thu Dec 26, 2024 5:08 am Allansfirebird wrote: Didn't Shikinami have a red flip phone in Eva 2.0 ?

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Jan 05, 2025 10:30 pm I think if she looks like Asuka that's what that film wants us to register her as. She is another character that has grown up and can live a normal life. The Groundworks back has the genga showing her call her Asuka too IIRC.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Mon Jan 06, 2025 8:17 am kuribo-04 wrote: I think if she looks like Asuka that's what that film wants us to register her as. She is another character that has grown up and can live a normal life. The Groundworks back has the genga showing her call her Asuka too IIRC.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jan 06, 2025 8:37 am I can't see Asuka saying Hi or Rei or Kaworu didn't. Especially since she and Shinji already had this huge cathartic denouement conversation.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Mon Jan 06, 2025 9:14 am AuraTwilight wrote: I can't see Asuka saying Hi or Rei or Kaworu didn't. Especially since she and Shinji already had this huge cathartic denouement conversation.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Konja7 » Mon Jan 06, 2025 11:05 am kuribo-04 wrote: I think if she looks like Asuka that's what that film wants us to register her as. She is another character that has grown up and can live a normal life. The Groundworks back has the genga showing her call her Asuka too IIRC.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Konja7 » Mon Jan 06, 2025 11:26 am This is the Groundwork for the Train Station scene: As we can see, the design for the redhead woman doesn't even appear in the Groundwork. Honestly, this just convince me more than the redhead woman was a last minute addition, since the designs for the two female students and the salaryman appear in this Groundwork.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Mon Jan 06, 2025 12:28 pm Konja7 wrote: As we can see, the design for the redhead woman doesn't even appear in the Groundwork. I don't think the Groundwork mentioned Asuka in that scene. Honestly, this just convince me more than the redhead woman was a last minute addition, since the designs for the two female students and the salaryman appear in this Groundwork.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Jan 10, 2025 4:04 am bros And this is besides the point anyway imo. It being Asuka is the obvious reading. And there's nothing contradicting it imo.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Fri Jan 10, 2025 5:41 am kuribo-04 wrote: bros And this is besides the point anyway imo. It being Asuka is the obvious reading. And there's nothing contradicting it imo.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Fri Jan 10, 2025 3:17 pm Weird_ocean wrote: That was a direction for animators, if you want someone to be drawn that looks exactly like Asuka, but not reveal the mystery of the ending, that's what I would do too. Just like with Kensuke and Asuka in instrumentality, when the direction was that they are "parent and a child" does not mean they are literally a father and a daughter. You need to consider how animation is made, it makes it easier for everybody who works on this movie, to just say it's Asuka, because there will be no additional questions or complications. But when you have two characters in the movie that look absolutely identical , and both look like Asuka, of course you will write "Asuka" as a direction. Especially if it doesn't matter for the context of the scene. But that doesn't mean that this is Shikinami.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Fri Jan 10, 2025 3:51 pm Axx°N N. wrote: Weird_ocean#944312 wrote: This is giving me "the date the pastor promised the world would end came and went but here's why he was still right" energy.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Fri Jan 10, 2025 4:38 pm Weird_ocean wrote: In animation, Anno can not tell what the actual story is to anyone, and still tell it.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Fri Jan 10, 2025 5:10 pm Axx°N N. wrote: Weird_ocean#944321 wrote: 2) I don't see what this has to do with Asuka's identity if the above establishes that it's more like a vision which passes. 3) See above.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Jan 11, 2025 4:52 am kuribo-04 wrote: bros And this is besides the point anyway imo. It being Asuka is the obvious reading. And there's nothing contradicting it imo.

[]: Re: I finally understand Shin Evangelion 3+1 ending and why Asuka is (NOT) on the train platform             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Jan 16, 2025 5:42 pm Konja7 wrote: The script mentions Kaworu and Rei. So, I would say Shinji is focusing on both characters.
